metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Media Arts 12 is the capstone media arts course, developing students as independent, conceptually sophisticated media artists. Students pursue a focused area of media art practice — photography, film, digital design, or animation — developing a substantial portfolio that demonstrates mastery, personal vision, and cultural awareness. Students engage with advanced questions of media representation, artistic ethics, and the role of media art in society. The course prepares students for post-secondary study in film, design, digital arts, and media-related fields.
metadata.general_description: The capstone media arts course developing a focused, sophisticated portfolio, artistic leadership, and critical cultural awareness — preparing students for post-secondary media arts and design programs.

big_ideas.statement: Mastery in the arts involves sustained practice, critical reflection, and willingness to take creative risks.
big_ideas.elaboration: Graduating media artists understand that a distinctive voice and high-quality work emerge from disciplined practice, honest self-criticism, and the courage to experiment and fail productively.

big_ideas.statement: Artists contribute to their communities by sharing their work and developing the next generation of artists.
big_ideas.elaboration: Senior students share their expertise with the broader school community, contribute to collaborative media projects, and articulate their artistic values and vision.

big_ideas.statement: Skilled artists intentionally select and combine elements of their art form to express their vision.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students demonstrate sophisticated command of their chosen media art form — making complex, intentional choices about technique, form, and concept to realize a coherent artistic vision.

big_ideas.statement: Engaging with the arts expands our understanding of identity and culture.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students situate their own practice within broader cultural and historical contexts — understanding their work as part of a conversation with the history of media art and the communities they belong to.

competencies.raw: Create a substantial portfolio of original media artworks demonstrating mastery, personal vision, and conceptual depth; demonstrate professional-level technical skill in a focused media art form; curate and present a capstone portfolio with critical reflection.
i_can_statements: I can create a substantial, polished portfolio of original media artworks that demonstrates technical mastery and a distinctive personal voice.
I can demonstrate professional-level technical skill in at least one focused area of media art practice.
I can develop and articulate a personal artistic vision that guides a coherent body of work.
I can curate and present my portfolio with thoughtful selection, sequencing, and critical artist statement.
I can take on leadership roles in collaborative media art projects.

competencies.raw: Conduct sophisticated critical analysis of media artworks using advanced frameworks; articulate the relationship between artistic choices, cultural context, and social impact; present a reflective artist statement demonstrating growth and future artistic direction.
i_can_statements: I can conduct sophisticated critical analysis of media artworks using art historical, cultural, and conceptual frameworks.
I can articulate how my artistic choices reflect my values, identity, and cultural context.
I can write a compelling artist statement that situates my work within a broader artistic and cultural conversation.
I can reflect on my complete artistic development and set specific goals for post-graduation creative practice.

areas.name: Advanced Photography
areas.raw: Advanced photography: conceptual series, fine art, and documentary practice.
topics: Conceptual photography: using photography to explore complex ideas, emotions, and social themes
Fine art photography: developing a personal style and creating exhibition-quality prints
Documentary and photojournalism: ethics, storytelling, and responsible representation
Advanced editing and manipulation: compositing, retouching, and creative post-processing
Portfolio series: creating a cohesive, thematically unified photographic series
Exhibition and publication: presenting photography for public audiences and professional contexts

areas.name: Advanced Film and Video
areas.raw: Advanced filmmaking, documentary production, and cinematic storytelling.
topics: Advanced cinematography: camera movement, lens choice, and visual language development
Directing: working with subjects/actors, communicating vision, and leading a production team
Documentary filmmaking: long-form non-fiction narrative, ethics, and editorial responsibility
Advanced editing: narrative structure, pacing, and sophisticated post-production
Sound design and original score: creating immersive audio environments for film
Film festival preparation and submission: presenting work for public and juried audiences

areas.name: Advanced Digital Design
areas.raw: Advanced graphic design, brand identity, and publication design.
topics: Advanced typography: expressive typographic design and typographic hierarchy
Brand identity systems: comprehensive visual identity — logo, colour, type, and application
Editorial and publication design: magazine, book, and zine layout at a professional level
Motion design: advanced animation and kinetic typography for digital media
User experience and interface design: principles of UX/UI for web and app contexts
Design for social impact: pro bono design projects for community organizations

areas.name: Capstone Portfolio and Artist Statement
areas.raw: Portfolio development, artist statement, and professional preparation.
topics: Portfolio curation: selecting, sequencing, and presenting a coherent body of work
Artist statement: articulating artistic vision, process, influences, and intentions
Professional presentation: portfolio websites, print folios, and digital presentation formats
Post-secondary pathways: portfolio requirements for film, design, digital arts, and animation programs
Industry connections: guest speakers, studio visits, and professional mentorship
Capstone exhibition: planning, designing, and presenting a public display of senior work

areas.name: Media Art, Society, and Ethics
areas.raw: Advanced media art criticism, ethics, and cultural leadership.
topics: Media art and power: how media art challenges or reinforces dominant narratives
Indigenous media sovereignty: First Peoples artists asserting cultural self-determination through digital art
Ethics of representation: consent, privacy, cultural sensitivity, and responsible storytelling
Artificial intelligence and art: AI-generated imagery, authorship, and the future of creative work
Environmental considerations: the carbon footprint of digital production and sustainable practice
Artist as citizen: the social responsibility of media artists in a democratic society
